qas.q: Configure Static Routes
qas.a: ip route (destination network) (destination subnet mask) (exit interface)

qas.q: Configure Default Routes
qas.a: ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 (exit interface)

qas.q: Steps to configure OSPFv2
qas.a:
1. create OSPF and name process ID
2. create router-id, such as 1.1.1.1
3. create network statement(s) for area
4. prevent routing updates on LAN interfaces
5. optional - OSPF cost
6. optional - bandwidth

qas.q: Steps 1-4: Configure OSPFv2
qas.a:
R2(config)# router ospf 10 (process ID)
R2(config-router)# router-id 2.2.2.2
R2(config-router)# network 192.168.10.104 0.0.0.3 area 0
R2(config-router)#passive-interface g0/0

qas.q: Steps 5-6: Configure OSPF cost of link and bandwidth
qas.a:
R2(config)# int s0/0/0
R2(config-if)# ip ospf cost 7500
R2(config-if)# bandwidth 128

qas.q: Steps to configure VLANs on switches
qas.a:
1. name VLANs
2. configure trunk ports
3. configure access ports
4. shut down unused ports
5. configure management VLAN 101

qas.q: Step 1. Name VLANs on switches
qas.a:
S1(config)# vlan 45
S1(config-vlan) name finance

qas.q: Step 2. Configure trunk ports on switches
qas.a:
S1(config)# int g1/1
S1(config-if)# switchport mode trunk
S1(config-if)# no shut

qas.q: Step 3. Configure access ports on switches
qas.a:
S1(config)# int f0/10
S1(config-if)# switchport mode access
S1(config-if)# switchport access vlan 45
S1(config-if)# no shut

qas.q: Step 4. Configure unused ports as access ports and shut down
qas.a:
S1(config)# int range f0/1-9, f0/11-14, f0/16-24
S1(config-if-range)# switchport mode access
S1(config-if-range)# shutdown

qas.q: Step 5. Configure management VLAN 101
qas.a:
S1(config)#ip default-gateway 192.168.101.1
S1(config)#int vlan 101
S1(config-if)#ip address 192.168.101.2 255.255.255.0

qas.q: Steps to configure DHCP Server
qas.a:
1. exclude range of static addresses
2. configure address pool name
3. define address range with network statement
4. define default gateway
5. optional - define dns server

qas.q: Step 1: Exclude static address on DHCP Server
qas.a: R1(config)#ip dhcp excluded-address 192.168.45.1 192.168.45.20

qas.q: Step 2: Create the address pool name for the DHCP server
qas.a: R1(config)#ip dhcp pool VLAN45

qas.q: Step 3: Use a network statement to define the DHCP server with the range of available addresses
Steps 4 & 5: Define default gateway & dns server on DHCP Server
qas.a:
R1(dhcp-config)#default-router 192.168.45.1
R1(dhcp-config)#dns-server 192.168.18.100

qas.q: Steps to configure static NAT
qas.a:
1. create mapping between inside local and inside global addresses
2. configure interface as inside
3. configure interface as outside

qas.q: Step 1: Translate inside source static local address to inside global address - NAT
qas.a: R2(config)#ip nat inside source static 192.168.18.46 198.51.100.14

qas.q: Steps 2 & 3: Configure interfaces participating in the translation as inside (connects to inside network) or outside (connects to outside network) relative to NAT.
qas.a:
R2(config)#interface g0/0
R2(config-if)#ip nat inside
R2(config)#interface s0/1/0
R2(config-if)#ip nat outside

qas.q: Steps to configure dynamic NAT
qas.a:
1. define pool of inside global addresses
2. configure a standard ACL to permit inside local addresses to be translated
3. bind the ACL to the pool
4. configure inside interfaces
5. configure outside interfaces

qas.q: Step 1: Configure a pool of public addresses for dynamic NAT
qas.a: R2(config)#ip nat pool INTERNET 198.51.100.3 198.51.100.13 netmask 255.255.255.240

qas.q: Step 2: Configure a standard access-list (ACL) to permit addresses to be translated using dynamic NAT
qas.a: R2(config)# access-list 1 permit 192.168.45.0 0.0.0.255

qas.q: Step 3: Bind the ACL to the pool - identify which devices (list) receive which addresses (pool)
qas.a: ip nat inside source list 1 pool INTERNET

qas.q: Steps 4 & 5: Identify interfaces participating in the translation as inside (connects to inside network) or outside (connects to outside network)
qas.a:
int s0/0/0
ip nat inside
int s0/1/0
ip nat outside

qas.q: Steps to create a named standard ACL
qas.a:
1. create named standard ACL
2. create permit or deny statement
3. link ACL to an interface

qas.q: Step 1: Configure a named standard ACL, use MANAGE
qas.a: R2(config)# ip access-list standard MANAGE

qas.q: Step 2: create permit or deny standard ACL statement
qas.a:
R2(config-std-nacl)# permit host 203.0.113.18
R2(config-std-nacl)# deny any
R2(config-std-nacl)# exit

qas.q: Step 3: apply ACL to an interface - IN (as packets enter into the interface) or OUT (as packets exit the interface)
qas.a:
R2(config)# int s0/1/0
R2(config-if)# ip access-group MANAGE IN

qas.q: Standard ACL rules:
qas.a:
1. used to permit or deny traffic only from source IPv4 addresses
2. to permit/deny 1 host, use address + wildcard, or host + address
3. to permit/deny all hosts, use any
4. ACL is applied inbound or outbound on the router interface closest to the destination of the traffic

qas.q: Rules to create an extended ACL:
qas.a:
1. create & name access-list - use both host & destination address and wildcard
2. keyword host can be used before address so that you don't have to state the wildcard
3. keyword any can be used instead of host source address or instead of destination address
4. put a protocol after deny/permit, such as tcp
5. if you want to permit traffic back to the host from a website, use keyword established in the statement
6. an extended ACL is applied inbound or outbound on the router interface that is closest to the source of the traffic
